title: phacelia cicutaria var. heterosepala
intro: Phacelia cicutaria var. heterosepala, also known as Lopscale or Cut-leaved Phacelia, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Phacelia cicutaria var. heterosepala can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
